text:
Peyton Manning is headed to Paris.

The formerNFLquarterback and two-time Super Bowl champion will be a part ofNBC’s Olympics coverageduring the Paris 2024 opening ceremony along the River Seine, he announced on the “Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon” on Tuesday. Manning, the five-time NFL MVP, will be on the broadcast alongside NBC Sports’ voice of “Sunday Night Football” Mike Tirico and host Kelly Clarkson.

Manning said he wouldn’t have considered this opportunity if it weren’t for Tirico.

Advertisement

“I kind of argued with him as to why he and NBC thought I was worthy,” Manning toldThe Athletic’s Andrew Marchand. “He said they were bringing in Kelly Clarkson to kind of give the perspective of a fan and wanted me to share the perspective of what these athletes are thinking about it.”

Manning, 47, played in the NFL from 1998 to 2015 with theIndianapolis Colts(1998 to 2011) andDenver Broncos(2012 to 2015). In addition to his Super Bowls and MVPs, Manning was a 14-time Pro Bowler, a seven-time first-team All-Pro, a four-time NFL touchdowns leader and a three-time NFL passing yards leader.

.@kellyclarkson, Peyton Manning and@miketiricowill be hosting the@NBCOlympicsopening ceremony!#FallonTonightpic.twitter.com/bz5FJMdHeq

— The Tonight Show (@FallonTonight)March 13, 2024



In his post-football career, Manning founded Omaha Productions, a media and entertainment company known for producing the alternative live broadcast of ESPN’s “Monday Night Football” called the “ManningCast,” hosted by Peyton and Eli Manning. The “ManningCast” debuted in September 2021.

The Paris 2024 opening ceremony will be the first to not occur in a stadium. Instead, 90 boats carrying athletes from numerous countries will float down the Seine ending at the Eiffel Tower. However, the opening ceremony will be scaled back in terms of capacity and ticketing amid security concerns in the city.

According to French Interior Minister Gérald Darmanin, 104,000 spectators of the open-air event will be paying ticket holders along the lower riverbanks, with another 222,000 spectators on the upper banks watching for free, according to multiple reports. However, the free tickets will not be available to the general public, and will instead be invitation-only.

The opening ceremony will take place on July 26 at 1:30 p.m. ET (7:30 p.m. Paris time).

Read more about Peyton Manning on the NBC Olympics broadcast inThe Athletic’s Andrew Marchand’s latest piece.

He also has extensive play-by-play broadcasting experience.Follow Lukas on Twitter@Weesesports
title: Peyton Manning to be on NBC’s Paris 2024 Olympics opening ceremony broadcast - The Athletic
author: Lukas Weese
description: Manning will join Mike Tirico and Kelly Clarkson on NBC's opening ceremony broadcast.
